rect.data-info: Index lookup on oi using idx_order (order_id=o.id) · Self: 6 ms (44.1%) · Rows: 3 · Loops: 2,400 · Table: order_items · Index: idx_order · Access: index · Complexity: O(log n + k) · Index lookup (ref): one B+tree descent then a sequential walk over k matching index entries. k = matching rows for the i… · Est.rows: 10 ⚠ Optimizer overestimate

rect.data-info: Single-row index lookup on u using PRIMARY (id=o.user_id) · Self: 1 ms (11.2%) · Rows: 1 · Loops: 2,400 · Table: users · Index: PRIMARY · Access: index · Complexity: O(log n) · Unique-key lookup (eq_ref): one B+tree descent into the inner table per outer row — as fast as MySQL can get. · Est.rows: 1

Index lookup on o using idx_status (status='delivered'), with index condition: (o.status = 'delivered') · Self: 1 ms (10.8%) · Rows: 2400 · Loops: 1 · Table: orders · Index: idx_status · Access: index · Complexity: O(log n + k) · Index lookup (ref): one B+tree descent then a sequential walk over k matching index entries. k = matching rows for the i… · Est.rows: 2400
Stream results · Self: 1 ms (8.0%) · Total: 10 ms · Rows: 8000 · Loops: 1 · Access: stream · Est.rows: 24326 ⚠ Optimizer overestimate

Group aggregate: count(distinct orders.id), sum(tmp_field) · Self: 0.991 ms (7.6%) · Total: 13 ms · Rows: 1 · Loops: 1 · Access: aggregate · Complexity: O(n log n) (typical) · Sort-based aggregation: MySQL sorts by the grouping key before collapsing duplicates.

Nested loop inner join · Self: 0.431 ms (3.3%) · Total: 9 ms · Rows: 8000 · Loops: 1 · Access: join · Complexity: O(n · log m) · Indexed nested loop: each outer row probes the inner table via an index descent. · Est.rows: 24326 ⚠ Optimizer overestimate

g.text: exponential — rare at run time, real at plan time

svg.text: select u.country AS country,count(distinct testdb.orders.id) AS orders,sum(tmp_field) AS revenue
Bar width = self-time (time in this operation only). Darker bars = slower.
Where a warning applies: hover the bar — it will show “In Query Analysis” and the info panel lists the bar label.
index_condition_pushdown=on — Parts of the WHERE clause are evaluated inside the storage engine using index columns before fetching the full row — reduces rows handed to the server
2 sort operation(s) — 8000 rows; may use disk-based filesort
• Increase sort_buffer_size or add an ordered index to avoid filesort
